Alumnus: Nate Browne - Theatre Major 

Nate shares some fun memories from his experience as a theatre major in the Artios Conservatory.

Check out what some of our Conservatory classes have been working on! "Artios Reads" is a collaborative project between four different classes to take classic and original children stories and turn them into short audio books for Artios kids! ❤️⠀
⠀
The Advanced Acting class pivoted their class during e-learning to focus on voice acting.⠀
⠀
The Literary Arts Classes curated and wrote classic and original stories. ⠀
⠀
The Advanced Theory class composed and recorded the intro music.⠀
⠀
The Graphic Design class created the logo! ⠀
⠀
Check out the first story premiering tomorrow! It's "The Princess and the Pea" read by Natalie Davis! #artiosaliveandwell
Check out this interview we did with  one of our recent graduates. Xandee shares about her experience at Artios as a Dance Major in our Conservatory program!

Alumnus: Micah Florian - Film Major

Last one (for now). Listen to Micah talk about his love for film that sparked at Artios and is now being furthered in his career at a media production company. Micah is super talented so be sure to check out his stuff on YouTube!

We are thrilled to present our first installment of “Artios Reads”. This short story, The Princess and the Pea, was recorded by advanced acting student, Natalie Davis! We hope you enjoy! #ArtiosReads #ArtiosAliveandWell

The Junior High cast of Wind in the Willows has been hard at work—and having a blast along the way! From wild adventures to unexpected friendships, this show is full of heart, humor, and a little bit of mischief. 🐸🚗💨

Don’t miss your chance to see the fun unfold on stage! 🎭✨

🎟️ Tickets are available now—but they’re going fast! Link in bio.

#WindInTheWillows #JuniorHighTheater #TheaterMagic

🎭✨ The journey begins! Our High School Drama Club just had their Big Fish kickoff boot camp—diving into their first full read-through and beginning the exciting process of bringing this story to life! 🐠💫

This behind-the-scenes look was captured and partially edited by our talented High School Production Club! 🎥👏 Stay tuned as they prepare for an unforgettable show.

#BigFish #ArtiosDrama #BehindTheScenes #ProductionClub #HighSchoolMusical #TheatreMagic

The first two performances of “Jane Eyre” are complete and were a fantastic success! These students and creative team have worked so hard and it is paying off in this beautiful and timeless production. 🕯️

2 more shows today and 3 more next week!

🎭 The curtains are up, the lights are shining, and Peter and the Starcatcher is officially running! 🌟

Opening weekend is here, and the magic is in full swing—don’t miss your chance to experience this unforgettable adventure! 🏴‍☠️✨ See how Peter becomes the boy who never grows up, how Hook gets his hook 🪝, and how a Starcatcher-in-training named Molly changes everything.

Remaining Shows:

	•	Saturday, November 16: 2:00 PM & 7:00 PM
	•	Thursday, November 21: 7:00 PM
	•	Friday, November 22: 7:00 PM
	•	Saturday, November 23: 5:00 PM

🎟 Tickets are selling fast—don’t miss out! Visit the link in our bio to grab yours before they’re gone. Some shows are already SOLD OUT! 🚨

#PeterAndTheStarcatcher #ArtiosAcademies #LiveTheatre #SugarHillEvents #PirateAdventure

From concept to chocolate! 🍫 In Intro to Creative Technology, our students took their creativity to the next level by designing and crafting their own chocolate bar molds. Starting with 3D modeling in Fusion 360, they transformed ideas into reality with vacuum forming and, finally, delicious chocolate. Hands-on projects like this help them explore new tools and stretch their creative boundaries.

#CreativeTech #3DPrinting #HandsOnLearning #Innovation

🍂🎉 Our annual Fall Festival was an unforgettable evening! Huge shoutout to our amazing parent volunteers who made it all possible. We had a pie tasting contest, chili cook-off, arts and crafts, games, and a DJ setting the vibe. Plus, ice cream from @jukenjivecreamery and the chance to Pie Your Principal! 🥧😆

Thanks to everyone who joined us for a night filled with fun and community. Already looking forward to next year! #FallFestival #SchoolSpirit #CommunityFun

A look inside PLOT TWIST 2024! 🎉🎉 

“The Artios community didn’t know they needed to see John Lane in a bald cap.” - Emily Sterner

🚨 Scavenger Hunt Fun Alert! 🚨

Our 3rd through 6th grade Drama Clubs embarked on an epic adventure to uncover the titles of their spring shows! 🕵️‍♂️ With clues in hand and excitement in the air, both groups pieced together the mystery—our 3rd and 4th graders will be setting sail in How I Became a Pirate 🏴‍☠️, while our 5th and 6th graders are ready to take flight in Peter Pan! 🧚‍♂️✨

Check out the video to see the magic unfold as these talented students discover their upcoming performances. We can’t wait to bring these stories to life this spring! 🎭

#ArtiosAcademies #DramaClub #ScavengerHunt #HowIBecameAPirate #PeterPan #SpringShows #TheatreMagic #youngperformersofamerica

Join us for a Prospective Family Open House at Artios of Sugar Hill! 🥳 At Artios, we see each child as uniquely created in God's image and integrate Biblical principles into our arts and academics to nurture their hearts. Experience our loving, Christ-centered community with an opportunity to tour our building and hear about our philosophy of education from our directors on July 11th and July 25th, at 6pm. RSVP through the link in our bio. We look forward to welcoming your family! 🎉

🌟 Junior High Project Showcase! 🌟 We are excited to share the final project from our Junior High 3D Design and 3D Printing II class: Arduino Controlled Cars! 🚗💡 Guided by Mr. Daws, our students designed, 3D-printed, and programmed their own remote-controlled cars using Arduino, featuring IR sensors, proximity sensors, and motors. This project combined electronics, coding, and design, giving students hands-on experience and practical skills. Check out the video to see their hard work in action! 🎥✨ #STEMEducation #ArduinoProjects #FutureEngineers #HandsOnLearning

What a year it’s been! A huge thank you @jeremiahdaws for compiling some of the best moments from our 2023-2024 school year. 🤩

You are a uniquely special group of young adults who have all loved and served our community well and we can’t wait to see how the Lord continues to guide, grow, bless, and use each and every one of you for His glory as you move on into the next season of life!! 🥳

Here’s our last SNEAK PEAK before the premier tonight!! Who’s ready?? 👏🥳

SNEAK PEAK ‼️ — Behind the Seas: Tech week on the Little Mermaid — premiering this Friday!

⚠️ Just a few tickets left between the 6pm and 8pm showings. Get ‘em before their gone!

Step into the spotlight with us as we unveil a captivating journey behind the scenes of Artios Academies of Sugar Hill’s latest theatrical production—The Little Mermaid. 🎥This feature-length documentary is your exclusive pass to witness the passion, dedication, and camaraderie that transform long days and late nights into an enchanting spectacle. ⭐️ From heartfelt moments to delightful shenanigans, experience the pulse of creativity, collaboration, and genuine connection as we showcase why this event is the pinnacle of the Artios experience.

A suggested donation of $10 per 🎟️ will contribute to enhancing our Film Department and allow us to obtain cutting-edge equipment like cinema lenses. These lenses are essential for producing short films, documentaries, promotional pieces, and enhancing the filming quality for upcoming shows. 

⚠️ Limited seating. ⚠️ Get your 🎟️ now!

Yesterday was a busy day for our HS students competing at BJU this week! Take a peak inside Day 2‼️

BJU Day 1 is in the books! 🥳🥳

Artios SH students got settled in their doors, toured the Bob Jones University campus in Greenville, SC, and had the chance to connect with other Artios students and faculty from other Artios locations around the country!

Day 2 will bring a full day of competitions for all participants. Please join us in praying for the students as they step out of their comfort zones and showcase the gifts God has given them. May they learn and grow individually (and collectively!) from the experience this week.

Stay tuned. #BJU2023

A little English line dancing in PE class today! 👏

Last week in Elem. Art: 
Our students began to focus on LINES by drawing trees! The students learned 3 ways to create branches: Ys, Xs, and Ws. They studied some famous paintings of trees and identified their letter shapes. Then, they went outside to observe real-life trees and practice their own line-making by sketching what they saw. 🌳

Next week, the students will take their sketches and turn them into paintings that will interpret the “Tree of Knowledge” from the Garden of Eden as a part of their study in the Ancient History time period this year!

Video Editing II class learning to edit faster. They know how to edit, but today, they were learning to make creative decisions quickly. They had 20 minutes to cut a 30 second video.  There was a lot of growth in this one exercise! 👏
Here’s what happens when the Upper Elem. Drama Club students find out they’re doing FINDING NEMO for their Spring musical!! 🎣🐠🐟

This year, our Artios-wide Scripture is John 10:27 and our staff and teachers could not be more excited! In today’s culture, there are so many “voices” competing for our attention on a daily basis, and this verse reminds us to put that attention back on CHRIST and His Word, the only voice that truly matters! #Artios2023

And just like that, we have officially kicked off a new year of Artios with the Junior High Field Day! 🎉💯 Our JH students and teachers had a blast getting to know each other over yummy food, crazy games, worship and more, before going into the start of classes next week! Next up.... High School Field Day! 🔥🔥
